text: Now that it’s over, I find myself lingering, suspended between gratitude and curiosity. There’s an end-of-ritual hush here: the neon glow and silent glass seem to hold something back, inviting one last look inward. I am filled with a quiet wonder, tinged by the sense that this departure is both an ending and a new question. What remains when the lights dim and the doors close? I leave thoughtful, slightly altered by what was shown and, especially, by what was left unsaid.
